rationale: The query/hyp engine is a linear-stability (Turing-type) analysis on a fixed network: perturbations projected onto graph-Laplacian eigenmodes yield a dispersion relation, and once a feedback-gain ratio crosses threshold the homogeneous state bifurcates into two degree-selected clusters, matched by a mean-field closure over degree classes. The candidate instead produces clustering via adaptive rewiring of the network topology itself (homophily-driven link changes and percolation effects), with no eigenmode/dispersion analysis, no bifurcation threshold, and no degree-based mean-field prediction — a different mechanism behind superficially similar words ("network", "consensus", "cluster formation"), so method_coherence is false. The candidate's object is opinion/consensus dynamics on adaptive social networks, genuinely different from the query's activator-inhibitor reaction-diffusion (Turing) patterns on random networks, so object_difference is true.
